item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Christ, this is the mecha you both wanna be in and not wanna be in.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"From the red corner, weighting 20,659.5186 pound from SCP Foundation, the jumbo mantis shrimp, SCP-1569!!"

+1

item.title: Re: Mixed feelings
item.content:encoded: There is absolutely no reason to connect this to rape.

item.title: Mixed feelings
item.content:encoded:
On the one hand, this is wonderfully written and legitimately disturbing, but fascinating at the same time.

On the other hand it's a tentacle-rape shrimp-car built for punching sharks.

…actually, those are both reasons to like it.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Noting that SPC-682 now exists and backlinks here.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: I have an idea based on something you said to me once, we should start a conspiracy.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Also with the new GOI guidelines, I think there may be a hub soon.

Indeed. Perhaps I will resurrect that silver of a concept and buff it up a great deal, but that will take some time to perfect.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
I have a draft that is more or less ready to drop as soon as I resolve some technical problems with it that directly references these shrimp in the Centre's universe.

Also with the new GOI guidelines, I think there may be a hub soon.

Also don't forget SPC-2615.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Author vetoed the proposal below and has opted to include their own crosslink. Timer has been removed as a result.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

Chances are, those who like this skip because of how it treats the Shark Punching Center are going to like SPC-140 which takes a similar approach to this group of interest. As such, I am recommending the following edit:

Changing this:

SCP-1569 is an organism physically resembling the peacock mantis shrimp (Odontodactylus scyllarus); however, it is of abnormally large size and proportionally high mass (9371 kg). SCP-1569's body is one meter tall, two meters wide, and nine meters long. The legs of SCP-1569 measure between two to three meters in length. A majority of the surface of SCP-1569's body is a reddish brown and composed of dense hydroxyapatite. Unlike non-anomalous peacock mantis shrimp, the bright neon green carapace covers the entirety of SCP-1569's thorax. The carapace is of unknown composition, as its durability has prevented sampling. Scratched onto the forefront of the carapace is a marking of a fist superimposing a sunburst. It is assumed this marking is not natural and was caused by something of anomalous origin due to the Foundation's inability to damage the carapace.

To this:

SCP-1569 is an organism physically resembling the peacock mantis shrimp (Odontodactylus scyllarus); however, it is of abnormally large size and proportionally high mass (9371 kg). SCP-1569's body is one meter tall, two meters wide, and nine meters long. The legs of SCP-1569 measure between two to three meters in length. A majority of the surface of SCP-1569's body is a reddish brown and composed of dense hydroxyapatite. Unlike non-anomalous peacock mantis shrimp, the bright neon green carapace covers the entirety of SCP-1569's thorax. The carapace is of unknown composition, as its durability has prevented sampling. Scratched onto the forefront of the carapace is a marking of a fist superimposing a sunburst. It is assumed this marking is not natural and was caused by something of anomalous origin due to the Foundation's inability to damage the carapace.

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Gah, I hate this.

Not the Skip, mind you. No, anything but–a bioorganic Mantis Shrimp Mecha used by the Shark Punching Centre to hit sharks (and associated aquatic minsters if that reality) in the face with about 15'000 newtons of force? That is fucking awesome, if I am to say the truth and if it were just that, I would have upvoted this eagerly.

The problem though, and I seem to be the only here with it, came from the interview log. Now, I understand that the Foundation has access to a lot of top quality things, be that devices or personnel. If you want to get better, there's very few else people you can turn to for some kf the best if the best, and I have no doubt they worked rather well on that D-class to make him suitable for an interview.

But the way he described it… The guys just says, almost casually, how he could fele his eyeballs pop and turn into mush around his face, and just… Goes on? No sign of that being traumatizing, no silence, nothing? He just… Says it? That really broke my SoD, and in the most depressing way because everything else about this Skip is just perfect!

And I know, that would be simply my reading of it, because text can be interpreted in so many ways and dialouge more so then anything else. But something about that interview log just rubbed me the wrong way, and that just throws me out of it. So, as much as it pains me to do this, I'm gonna have to give it a Neutral-Vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's also a Shark-Punching Conveyance, and a Symbiotic Personnel Carrier. :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
In the interest of providing some (possibly lacking) outsider opinion: I had no idea what the Shark Punching Center was until I read the comments, but this SCP was an easy upvote. Granted, I'm probably more lenient than most of you that have been around longer (I tend to upvote SCPs I find above average, rather than strictly exceptional) but I think this one stands on its own, Shark Punching Center or not.

What got me was the smooth integration of genuine body horror with the more affectionate and sympathetic components of the mantis shrimp's behavior and the non-hostile yet dangerous aspects of the host-vehicle relationship. I didn't find it funny per se, but it feels like it stands out in being an interesting and relevant read, without going to the extremes other SCPs go to in order to evoke dread or simply go straight for the "simply weird" flavor. In the end, I felt sorry for the shrimp being alone, the previous pilot ending up the way he did (especially considering the "affectionate behaviors") and I liked that the D-Class was quite level-headed for someone who had just had his eyes gouged out by a giant shrimp. I was a lot more invested than most SCPs that have similar elements, so for me, something's different (and better) here.

I generally like articles that feel more reserved, have a level tone and imply more than they say. Well written and more original than most, +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I definitely agree with you on that. The whole explanation of what happened to the D-class with the tentacles was absolutely terrifying. Nearly puked up my lunch.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I LOVE this. First, the skip itself is great, I've always thought the mantis shrimp needed it's own SCP. Secondly, making it SPC only makes it better for me. I thought there was something off about it hating sharks, but then it hit me, since mantis shrimp 'punch' their prey.
Making the guy freak out over the mantis shrimp's extended color field was a nice touch, too
+100million from me

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
1. This is a Shark-Punching Crustacean. Am I the first to notice this?
  2. I don't actually mind the Shark Punching Center in a serious SCP. I know that they're a site in-joke and many people don't find them funny, but I can honestly believe that they exist in some parallel universe and are taken completely seriously. I just don't think this SPC and the ridiculous SPC that gets posted in -Js are the same. I mean, what if they exist in a parallel universe where sharks legitmately threaten human existence?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Zyn, I now love you and weizhong with all the passion of a mantis shrimp punch.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This reminds me of an idea for an SCP I had long ago. Gave me wonderful nostalgia.
But at the discovery that this was a biological vehicle, I was disturbed beyond belief.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Scratched onto the forefront of the carapace is a marking of a fist superimposing a sunburst.

And then I just lost it. Woke my son up through my shrieks of unbridled mirth. (I have a very well developed sense of the absurd. It makes me laugh.)

And I don't know if any of have watched this amazing video?

You should. You really really should.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Holy shit! You read the Oatmeal too?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This was a good article, especially the addendum that added not only disturbing body horror, but also a possible connection to SCP-1000…and then it's the Shark Punching Center.

No, I'm not going to say "oh well, it's still good" because it isn't. The SPC connection turned this into a joke, and it isn't funny. -1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think I would've liked it better without SPC, but yeah, +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I get this. It answers the question "why a mantis shrimp of all shrimps?" But they are also not known for taking down sharks, and therein lies the beauty.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "Zegana, the Legion will not desist in its pursuit of criminals just because they happen to venture underwater. Though the oceans of Ravnica may be your realm, the Simic show no interest in enforcing any laws, including natural ones, I may note. So if you won't allow us to deputize any merfolk, can you at least provide us with a means to operate beneath the waves? Flaming swords… don't work too well down there."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The body horror from the interview log…. oh God. +1 with entusiasm.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I laughed so hard at this, and honestly, they are still hella cute to me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sounds closer to Simic to me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Why in the hell would an organization dedicated to punching sharks use a shrimp as their underwater assault support?

Weizhong already mentioned it on the second page of this discussion section, but FRICKIN MANTIS SHRIMPS ARE MONSTERS. Beautiful, scary monsters. They extol death with the brilliance of dying stars. They are Genghis Khan bathed in sherbert ice cream.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
a shrimp vehicle

See, this is why I think it works, especially with the SPC reference. Why in the hell would an organization dedicated to punching sharks use a shrimp as their underwater assault support? It's wacky in ways that only make sense in 80's cartoons. So much to love!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I upvoted it because the writing is good…but it just reminded me so much of this piece done by The Oatmeal that it somewhat detracted a little.

As soon as you said a man came out of it, I could not stop thinking about that, and even more so when you said it was a biological vehicle.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1

I upvoted before I reached the comments. It was just so insane… a shrimp vehicle. I liked the touch on saving the D-Class for re-use in further experiments.

I liked the SPC reference, personally. I'm not sure if it was the driving force behind this or not, but I think anyone who downvotes just for that is being dense.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's exactly what I thought of too. The main reason I upvoted was that I chose to think that the symbol was the Boros rather than the SPC, and the thought that the Legion had some sort of weird shrimp-soldiers hidden away somewhere cracked me up.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
with the symbol of a fist superimposing a sunburst on the back

I didn't even think of SPC at first. I pictured it like this :

Boros Legion
Hide

That's a guild in Magic: the Gathering and that really distracted me from the article. xD

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The Foundation is now working under the hypothesis that SCP-1569 is a biological vehicle

Upvoted.

But I do agree with everyone else, the SPC thing really detracts the article's value… But it won't change my vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My thoughts exactly.

This left me wondering who that guy might be, from some sort of underwater civilization that can use biological vehicles, that blew my mind with all unanswered possibilities.

Then i come here and it's just Shark Punching Center.

Glad that the article itself is good enough to stand on it's own if this SPC garbage gets removed one day.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think downvoting an orthewise good article for having a rather harmless reference to an old gag is just a way to show that we are trying to impose our particular opinion over an article and its Author's vision of the skip.

It's not just a reference. The entire article is built around the Shark Punching Center being an actual thing that we're supposed to take seriously. Which is completely ridiculous.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I read this last night and didn't really like it enough to upvote, but I think I've now changed my mind. I only really have two substantial criticisms; first, the whole SPC thing. It's not explicitly mentioned in the article, only here in the discussion, so that's not too bad, but it still irks me. I think this would be a lot more interesting if it were just some vessel of inexplicable origin, instead of having a clear purpose. Secondly, and this is more superficial, the containment procedures are really short. I mean, bulking them out any further would just be padding for the sake of padding, but it still seems like too little. Maybe add a bit about cleaning procedures? Overall, though, I like this, so +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I upvoted this before I checked the comments and even though I hate the SPC there's no way I'm going to change my vote because of that.

I do feel the whole SPC things detracts a lot from any article though, it's never going to secure upvotes but will almost definitely secure at least a few downvotes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
igi

and, as such, +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: While people have the right to vote under whatever they feel it's good and bad in an article, I think downvoting an orthewise good article for having a rather harmless reference to an old gag is just a way to show that we are trying to impose our particular opinion over an article and its Author's vision of the skip. This is not, in any way, a complain towards anybody's standards and critique, but a way to point something I consider slightly pityful. I've probably done it myself before and being or not aware of it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: idgi

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I still think the SPC is funny.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The description of 1569-1's internals is a little confusing. There's a good deal of repetition that could be taken out, not to mention the storage organ can't be identical to the one in 1569 unless it's been sized down; don't want to leave something like that up to assumption.

I recommend starting addendum 02 off with the D class entering the carapace. The way it's structured made me question whether he was in there. And I'd also say the last note about what happened to him, beyond administration of amnestics, is superfluous.

But other than that, this is kind of a neat idea. One part Giant Animal, one part cute, one part Builder Bear. All Shark Punching CenterWait, this is actually from the Shark Punching Center? I'm not sure how to feel about that, being the only person on the site who still thinks the SPC is funny.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: SPC-related crap makes me that much more likely to downvote an article. The fact that I went with neutral should tell you what I thought of it, minus the shark stuff. Honestly, it's pretty great as-is and didn't need to have the unfunny-or-maybe-it's-no-longer-supposed-to-be-funny-and-actually-it's-an-alternate-universe-I-don't-know thing that is the SPC shoehorned in.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, you're right about the "not being funny" part, that's for sure. If it's trying to play the SPC thing completely straight, then it's a completely inane exercise in formally adapting a site in-joke into an article. I happen to disagree about that being a good thing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Except this article is not funny, and was not meant to be funny, and was not trying to be funny. And this is a good thing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I am really, really done with the whole Shark Punching Center thing. It ceased being funny about ten SPC-themed articles ago.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Tentacles, man. I actually did it. Thanks to Djoric for aiming me in this direction. A fair amount of this was crafted from feedback given by Randomini, MrCarbon, bluesoul, thedeadlymoose, Anborough, Slate, Pemander, and otherpeoplebutthislistisgettinglong.

+ What the tentacles do, since I'm sure somebody is still 'Bro tentacles wtf'
- Bro it's like  this

Nose - Breathing apparatus
Mouth - Food
Anus, genitalia - Removal of waste. You don't want to shit in your car shrimp mecha, do you?
Eyes - Vision

You know what's fun? This is a GOI skip. Most people haven't gotten it in the first read.

+ Spoiler for what GOI it is
- :D

SCP-1569 was designed and bred by the folks at the Shark Punching Center! The "fist superimposing a sunburst" is their logo! Also the thing punches other things, and hates sharks. Or at least the operator did. Who knows how this particular shrimp and operator ended up in the SCP reality.
